page.title: Marbella Family Boat Day with Snorkeling: Kid-Friendly Fun

data.meta_description: Plan a marbella family boat day snorkeling trip from Puerto Banús. Kid-friendly charter with skipper, drinks & snacks from €749/2h. Book your family adventure today.
data.summary: Discover a marbella family boat day snorkeling experience from Puerto Banús. Kid-friendly charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, snacks, and snorkeling gear—prices start at €749 for 2 hours.
key_facts: Family-friendly boat charters from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Prices from €749/2h (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) up to €4,719/4h (Mangusta 80 with jet ski).
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava), and snacks.
Snorkeling equipment provided on request at no extra cost.
Boats accommodate 5 to 12 passengers; ideal for small to large families.
Departures from Puerto Banús; explore Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and Cabopino coves.
Spanish IVA (21%) included in all prices.
Book via WhatsApp for boats not listed with fixed rates.

faq.q: What is included in a marbella family boat day snorkeling charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Snorkeling gear is provided free on request—just ask when booking.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a family with young children?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are excellent choices for small to medium families (up to 9–11 pax). They are stable, have shaded areas, and depart from Puerto Banús. For larger groups, the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 (up to 12 pax) offer more space and a flybridge for panoramic views.

faq.q: Can we bring our own snorkeling gear?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own gear. However, we provide snorkeling equipment free of charge on all our boats. Just let us know in advance so we can have it ready for your family.

faq.q: How long is a typical family boat day snorkeling trip?
faq.a: Most families choose a 4‑hour or 6‑hour charter. A 4‑hour trip lets you reach the best snorkeling coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, enjoy a swim, and have lunch on board. An 8‑hour trip allows time for a longer cruise toward Estepona or Sotogrande.

faq.q: Are there any age restrictions for children on the boat?
faq.a: No minimum age; children of all ages are welcome. Life jackets for all sizes are provided. The skipper will brief you on safety, and the calm Mediterranean waters near Marbella are ideal for little ones.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on our booked day?
faq.a: We monitor conditions closely. If the forecast is unsafe for a family trip, we offer a free reschedule or full refund. The skipper will also advise on alternative sheltered routes if the weather is marginal.

faq.q: Can we customise the itinerary for our family?
faq.a: Absolutely. The skipper is flexible and will follow your preferences. Popular family stops include the shallow coves of Cabopino, the crystal waters of Cala Cortés, or a slow cruise past the Golden Mile. Just discuss your wishes at departure.

faq.q: How do I book a marbella family boat day snorkeling charter?
faq.a: You can book directly via WhatsApp for any boat. For the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, you can also book online via our website. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during summer weekends.

data.body_html:
Looking for a marbella family boat day snorkeling adventure that everyone will remember? From the glamorous Puerto Banús, you can set sail on a private charter designed with families in mind. Our boats come with a licensed skipper, all drinks and snacks included, and snorkeling gear provided free. Prices start at €749 for a 2‑hour trip on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, making it easy to fit into any family holiday budget.

Why Choose a Family Boat Day in Marbella?

Marbella’s coastline is dotted with calm, shallow coves perfect for children. The water temperature averages 24°C in summer, and visibility often exceeds 15 metres—ideal for first‑time snorkelers. A private charter gives your family total flexibility: stop when you want, swim where you like, and eat when you’re hungry. No crowds, no queues, just your own floating base for the day.

Our Kid‑Friendly Fleet

We have boats to suit every family size. For smaller groups (up to 5), the Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m) are nimble and easy. For medium families (up to 10–11), the Astondoa 40 (12.5m) and Azimut 39 (12.5m) offer stable rides with shaded cockpits. Larger families or multi‑family groups (up to 12) will love the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) or Mangusta 80 (24m), which have plenty of deck space and a flybridge for panoramic views.

Best Snorkeling Spots Near Puerto Banús

Your skipper will recommend the day’s best spots based on weather and sea conditions. Favourite family locations include:

  • Cala del Faro – A sheltered cove with rocky reefs teeming with small fish.
  • Cala Cortés – Shallow, sandy bottom, perfect for beginners.
  • Cabopino – A nature reserve with crystal‑clear water and minimal boat traffic.
  • Río Verde – Calm waters near the Marbella coastline, great for a quick dip.

What’s Included in Your Charter

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Snorkeling masks, fins, and buoys are provided free on request. Just let us know when you book, and we’ll have everything ready.

Pricing Options for Families

BoatCapacityPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 409 pax€749€2,299
Azimut 3911 pax€749€2,299
Mangusta 80 (any)12 pax€4,719 (4h min)–
Other boats5–12 paxPricing on request via WhatsApp

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. For longer charters, simply add hours at a pro‑rata rate.

Sample Family Itinerary (4‑Hour Trip)

10:00 – Meet at Puerto Banús, welcome drinks and safety briefing.
10:15 – Depart towards Cabopino, cruising past the Golden Mile.
10:45 – Arrive at Cala Cortés. Snorkeling, swimming, and paddleboarding.
12:00 – Light lunch on board with drinks.
12:45 – Slow cruise back, spotting La Concha mountain.
13:00 – Return to Puerto Banús. The whole family will be smiling.

Tips for a Smooth Family Day

  • Bring sunscreen (SPF 50+), hats, and rash vests for little ones.
  • Pack a towel and a change of clothes.
  • Let the skipper know if anyone gets seasick—they can adjust speed and route.
  • Book a morning slot when the sea is calmest and the light is best for snorkeling.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a marbella family boat day snorkeling charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Snorkeling gear is provided free on request—just ask when booking.

Which boat is best for a family with young children?

The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are excellent choices for small to medium families (up to 9–11 pax). They are stable, have shaded areas, and depart from Puerto Banús. For larger groups, the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 (up to 12 pax) offer more space and a flybridge for panoramic views.

Can we bring our own snorkeling gear?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own gear. However, we provide snorkeling equipment free of charge on all our boats. Just let us know in advance so we can have it ready for your family.

How long is a typical family boat day snorkeling trip?

Most families choose a 4‑hour or 6‑hour charter. A 4‑hour trip lets you reach the best snorkeling coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, enjoy a swim, and have lunch on board. An 8‑hour trip allows time for a longer cruise toward Estepona or Sotogrande.

Are there any age restrictions for children on the boat?

No minimum age; children of all ages are welcome. Life jackets for all sizes are provided. The skipper will brief you on safety, and the calm Mediterranean waters near Marbella are ideal for little ones.

What happens if the weather is bad on our booked day?

We monitor conditions closely. If the forecast is unsafe for a family trip, we offer a free reschedule or full refund. The skipper will also advise on alternative sheltered routes if the weather is marginal.

Can we customise the itinerary for our family?

Absolutely. The skipper is flexible and will follow your preferences. Popular family stops include the shallow coves of Cabopino, the crystal waters of Cala Cortés, or a slow cruise past the Golden Mile. Just discuss your wishes at departure.

How do I book a marbella family boat day snorkeling charter?

You can book directly via WhatsApp for any boat. For the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, you can also book online via our website. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during summer weekends.
